scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE AT BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR, S/o {{FATHER'S_NAME}}, Aged about 45 years, R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL, S/o {{FATHER'S_NAME}}, Aged about 38 years, R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

1. The Plaintiff is an adult citizen of India, residing at the address mentioned in the cause title above. The Plaintiff is the lender who advanced the hand loan to the Defendant.

2. The Defendant is an adult citizen of India, residing at the address mentioned in the cause title above. The Defendant is the borrower who received the loan amount from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same.

3. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides within the territorial limits of this Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within these limits. The valuation of the suit for jurisdiction is Rs. 15,00,000/- which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Court.

CAUSE OF ACTION

4. The cause of action first arose on 15.03.2024 when the Plaintiff advanced the loan amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant via NEFT bank transfer.

5. The cause of action further arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant defaulted on the repayment of the loan amount, making the entire principal amount due and payable.

6. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant failed to repay the amount despite the legal notice dated 01.07.2024 sent by the Plaintiff demanding repayment.

FACTS

7. The Plaintiff and Defendant are known to each other. Based on the request made by the Defendant for financial assistance, the Plaintiff agreed to advance a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant.

8. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's bank account via NEFT. The transaction is identified by UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. There was no written agreement executed between the parties for this loan.

9. Subsequently, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same through WhatsApp messages exchanged with the Plaintiff.

10. The Defendant was obligated to repay the loan amount. However, the Defendant failed to repay the amount by the agreed timeline, resulting in a default on 20.06.2024.

11. The Plaintiff sent a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of the principal amount along with interest. The Defendant received the notice but failed to comply with the demand.

12. Despite repeated oral demands and the legal notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the principal amount or the interest accrued thereon. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the said amount from the Defendant.

STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

13. The statement of account regarding the loan transaction is as follows: Principal Amount Advanced: Rs. 15,00,000/- on 15.03.2024. Part Payments Received: Nil. Balance Principal Due: Rs. 15,00,000/-. Interest Claimed: 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till date of realization.

INTEREST

14. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/-. The interest is claimed for the period from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of realization. The Plaintiff seeks interest for the pre-suit period, pendente lite under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and future interest until full payment.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the loss caused by the breach of contract under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as obligated.

16. This suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as civil courts have jurisdiction to try all suits of a civil nature unless expressly barred.

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which empowers the Court to order interest on the principal sum adjudged from the date of the suit to the date of payment.

LIMITATION

18. This suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of demand/refusal. The suit is filed within three years from 01.07.2024 (date of legal notice).

COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT COMPLIANCE

19. The Plaintiff states that the suit value exceeds Rs. 3,00,000/- and thus falls under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The Plaintiff has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, or alternatively, seeks exemption from the same.

COURT FEE

20. The appropriate court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint under the relevant provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRA
